items.title: Brazil 2 Japan 1: Arsenal star Gabriel Martinelli scores last-gasp winner to fire South Americans into World Cup last-16
items.summary: SUPER-SUB Gabriel Martinelli fired Brazil into the World Cup last-16 with a dramatic last-gasp strike against Japan. The Arsenal star completed the turnaround with a composed effort in the fifth minute of added time. MORE TO FOLLOW

items.title: Germany v Paraguay: World Cup 2026 last 32 – live
items.summary: ⚽ Kick-off time: 4.30pm EDT/9.30pm BST/6.30am AET ⚽ Bracketology | Player guide | Golden Boot standings The Chloe Kelly Paradox is one of the features of this World Cup. Arsenal’s Gabriel Martinelli, one of the world’s best substitutes, has just scored an injury-time winner for Brazil against Japan.

items.title: Leeds Powerchair FC win Disability FA Cup with Maradona-like assist – video
items.summary: Leeds Powerchair FC beat Teesside Powerchair Football Club 3-1 to win their first EE Disability FA Cup. Mitchell Tinkler gave Teesside the lead before Leeds came back through their captain Dylan Kelsall, who scored two goals before setting up Dan Rigby to score the third Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal fans all demand Bruno Guimaraes transfer as he sets up Gabriel Martinelli for last-gasp World Cup last-32 winner
items.summary: ARSENAL fans are already dreaming of a link up between Bruno Guimaraes and Gabriel Martinelli after Brazil's last-gasp win. items.title: Man City star Rodri ‘set for surgery on mystery injury after Spain’s World Cup and will miss start of next season’
items.summary: RODRI is set to undergo surgery on a mystery injury after Spain's World Cup campaign, reports say. The 2024 Ballon d'Or winner played 33 times for Manchester City last season but did have spells out of the side with hamstring and groin problems. items.title: Thomas Tuchel admits he does NOT know his best England XI for World Cup knockouts amid double injury nightmare
items.summary: THOMAS TUCHEL will take “any win” because he claims this is the hardest World Cup ever. England were underwhelming again in Saturday’s 2-0 victory over Panama that confirmed their top-spot qualification. items.title: Kane likely to sign new Bayern deal despite Barcelona interest
items.summary: England captain Harry Kane is likely to sign a new deal to extend his stay at Bayern Munich despite interest from Barcelona.

items.title: Former NFL running back Johnson reveals MND/ALS diagnosis
items.summary: Chris Johnson, a former running back with the Tennessee Titans, New York Jets and Arizona Cardinals, has revealed he has been diagnosed with Motor Neurone Disease/Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis.

items.title: Fans claim David Beckham ‘looks fuming’ after being rushed home from World Cup for annual tradition
items.summary: FAMILY man David Beckham rushed back from the World Cup to honour an annual tradition, after England's win over Panama. But fans felt that he didn't look too happy about it. items.title: Liverpool target Diomande would prefer to join PSG
items.summary: Liverpool target Yan Diomande would prefer to join Paris St-Germain, as the French club aim to agree a deal with RB Leipzig for the Ivory Coast winger.

items.title: Casemiro SCORES just minutes after fans claimed Man Utd star’s World Cup performance vs Japan was ‘just sad’
items.summary: MANCHESTER UNITED'S departing star Casemiro silenced his critics with a crucial goal for Brazil after a dismal first half against Japan. items.title: Hastings avoids sanction for making contact with injured Bradford player
items.summary: Jackson Hastings has not been charged by the RFL's Match Review Panel (MRP) after he appeared to touch injured Bradford forward Will Gardiner during St Helens' 40-10 victory over the Bulls on Saturday.

items.title: ‘Ridiculous’ – World Cup fans raging after ‘pointless’ kit clash for Brazil vs Japan leaving viewers struggling
items.summary: FOOTBALL fans complained of a kit clash as Brazil took on Japan in the World Cup round-of-32 on Monday.
